examples.x: The new restaurant's food was fair to middling, not exceptional but not terrible either.
The team's performance in the match was fair to middling, showing some promise but lacking consistency.
The movie received fair to middling reviews, with critics expressing mixed opinions about its quality.
Her skills in playing the piano are fair to middling, showing improvement but not yet at an advanced level.
The company's financial results for the quarter were fair to middling, meeting expectations but not exceeding them.

word_list.pronunciation: lˈiːv ɐ lˈɑːt mˈʌtʃ təbi dɪzˈaɪɚd

senses.definition: to fail to fulfill one's expectations

examples.x: The new software's user interface leaves a lot to be desired, making it difficult to navigate.
The company's customer service leaves much to be desired, with long wait times and unhelpful responses.
The cleanliness of the hotel room left a lot to be desired, with visible dust and dirt.
His performance in the interview left much to be desired, and he didn't get the job.

senses.definition: in a very unfavorable or disadvantageous condition

examples.x: The old house was in bad nick, with crumbling walls and a leaking roof.
After neglecting regular maintenance, his car was in poor nick, constantly breaking down.
Due to a lack of exercise and a sedentary lifestyle, his fitness level was in terrible nick, leading to various health issues.
The abandoned garden was in bad nick, overgrown with weeds and devoid of any healthy plants.
The athlete's performance was in poor nick during the competition, showing signs of fatigue and lack of preparation.

senses.definition: used to say that a person or thing is not as good as other people say they are

examples.x: The new restaurant had received rave reviews, but the food was mediocre and not as delicious as it was cracked up to be.
The expensive designer handbag turned out to be of poor quality and not as durable as it was cracked up to be.
The celebrity's performance at the concert was lackluster; it wasn't as captivating as it was cracked up to be.
The job offer seemed promising, but the reality of the position was far less appealing than it was cracked up to be.
The highly anticipated movie didn't live up to the hype; it wasn't as exciting as it was cracked up to be.

senses.definition: a thing that is less desirable or has a lower quality or price than what was mentioned

examples.x: The public transportation system serves as the poor man's car, providing affordable transportation for those who cannot afford private vehicles.
The basic smartphone model is sometimes called the poor man's premium flagship devices with advanced features and technology.
The discount brand of coffee is the poor man's version of the high-end specialty coffees available at upscale cafes.
The imitation leather jacket he bought is often referred to as the poor man's genuine leather.
The local diner is considered the poor man's gourmet restaurant, offering affordable meals but lacking in culinary finesse.

senses.definition: in a very bad physical condition due to being used for an extended period of time

examples.x: After hiking through the rugged terrain, my shoes were worse for wear, with worn-out soles and tattered laces.
The antique book had been passed down through generations and was now worse for wear, with faded pages and a fragile spine.
He returned from the intense workout at the gym, drenched in sweat and worse for wear, feeling utterly exhausted.
The car had been driven for thousands of miles without proper maintenance and was now worse for wear, with engine troubles and a worn-out suspension.
The old wooden chair had served its purpose for decades but was now worse for wear, with chipped paint and a wobbly frame.

senses.definition: something that is done vey poorly

examples.x: The project was a complete dog's breakfast; the team failed to coordinate and the end result was a disorganized mess.
He attempted to cook a gourmet meal but ended up with a dog's dinner; the food was overcooked and the presentation was messy.
The party preparations were a dog's breakfast; decorations were haphazardly placed, and the schedule was poorly planned.
The report was filled with spelling errors, inconsistent formatting, and disjointed paragraphs; it was a real dog's breakfast.
The renovation work on the house was a dog's dinner; walls were left unpainted, wires were exposed, and the overall finish was shoddy.

senses.definition: used to suggest someone that a particular course of action or choice is better than what they think, even though it is still bad

examples.x: You could do worse than to follow her advice in this matter.
If you're looking for a reliable car, you could do worse than a Toyota.
When it comes to choosing a career path, you could do worse than pursuing a degree in engineering.
Considering the current situation, we could do worse than to invest in renewable energy.
In terms of finding a mentor, you could do worse than seeking guidance from someone with extensive experience in your field.

senses.definition: used to refer to something that cannot be restored to its original or previous condition

examples.x: The damage to the ancient artifact was beyond recall, despite the best restoration efforts.
Once the confidential information is leaked, it will be beyond recall, and the consequences could be severe.
The decision to close the factory is beyond recall now, as all the equipment has been dismantled.
The words spoken in anger were beyond recall, leaving a lasting impact on their relationship.
After the contract was signed, the terms became beyond recall, and any changes would require mutual consent.

senses.definition: (of a piece of clothing) looking shabby and worn-out

examples.x: His favorite pair of jeans became out at elbows after years of regular use, developing holes and signs of distress.
Last year, her dress was out at the elbows, exhibiting signs of wear with worn patches and loose threads.

senses.definition: in a depressed, bad, or weakened state

examples.x: Consumer confidence is currently at a low ebb due to economic uncertainties.
After the loss of her job, her spirits were at a low ebb for several months.
The team's morale was at a low ebb following a series of defeats.
The company's profits were at a low ebb, prompting a strategic reassessment.
During the recession, the country's economy was at a low ebb, leading to widespread hardship.

senses.definition: used to refer to machinary that is not functioning properly or at all

examples.x: The old printer is on the blink again; it's time to consider getting a new one.
Due to the extreme weather, the heating system in the house is on the blink.
The car's air conditioning is on the blink, making the summer drives quite uncomfortable.
The office coffee machine is on the blink, causing inconvenience for the employees.
The streetlights in the neighborhood have been on the blink for days, posing a safety concern.

senses.definition: unable to operate normally for a limited time

examples.x: The elevator is currently out of action due to maintenance work; please use the stairs.
After injuring his ankle during the game, the star player will be out of action for at least two weeks.
The computer system crashed, leaving the entire office out of action until the IT team resolves the issue.
The broken-down car on the side of the road was out of action, leaving the driver stranded.
Following the power outage, many homes were out of action without electricity for several hours.

senses.definition: (of a machine) to fail to work properly

examples.x: My old car finally gave up the ghost; it stopped running altogether and couldn't be fixed.
The ancient printer in the office gave up the ghost, leaving us unable to print any documents.
After years of reliable service, the washing machine finally gave up the ghost and had to be replaced.
The computer's hard drive started making strange noises before it eventually gave up the ghost, resulting in the loss of all data.
The old refrigerator in the kitchen finally gave up the ghost, leaving the food inside to spoil.
The town has become very down at heel.
After losing his job, he appeared down at heel, unable to afford new clothes.
